summary: O'Neil shares a brief personal update about recovering from illness and directs readers to a New York Magazine article about a reporter who infiltrated Kappa Beta Phi, a secretive Wall Street fraternity. The post serves primarily as a link recommendation rather than original analysis, though her framing ('Pretty amazing, if true') suggests both intrigue and skepticism about elite financial culture.
key_points: Wall Street has secretive social institutions like Kappa Beta Phi that operate outside public scrutiny
Investigative journalism can expose the hidden culture of financial elites
O'Neil positions herself as a curator of content critical of Wall Street excess
money_quotes: I have a whole backlog of rants and vents but no time this morning to even start
key_insight: Wall Street's secretive social institutions reveal a culture of elite insularity that deserves public scrutiny.
